channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: Temtem: Pioneers Unveils New Species-Wide Pedigree Perk System For The In-The-Works Monster-Collection Survival Game
item.content:encoded:
The already-successful Kickstarter campaign for Temtem: Pioneers, the multiplayer survival action title in the monster collection series, has unveiled a unique new quirk called the Pedigree, which unlocks perks across an entire Tem’s species. 

Pedigrees aren’t just tied to one individual Tem. Instead, the entire species will reap the benefits of that species’ pedigree, including passive buffs and stat increases, new Techniques, status condition applications, and more. All Tems picked up in the future of that same species will have the benefits, and they’re even retroactively applied to the ones you already have. 

An example of improved pedigree is shown in a GIF with the Banapi, a fire-type little guy who, with the pedigree, can inflict burns with basic attacks on an enemy. 

Pedigrees are improved through simple gameplay with that Tem involved—as the blog post explains, it can just be done through taming, breeding, defeating enemies, and letting them tag along to help harvest resources. Plus, interacting with them throughout the game will improve them as well. 

This information reveal blog is the latest bit of new information from the Kickstarter campaign, which wraps up in four days. We also saw a preview of the quite dynamic housing system last week, as well as several other new Tem species, including one inspired by mosquitoes. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/temtem-pioneers-unveils-new-species-wide-pedigree-perk-system-for-the-in-the-works-monster-collection-survival-game-2000137987
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Hytale Announces In-Game Custom Server Discovery Tool Coming In Update 5
item.content:encoded:
No more browsing through third-party directories or Discord servers for the many massive custom games in Hytale—Hypixel Studios has announced that it’s bringing an official in-game server discovery feature to the early access block survival title. 

Through the server discovery feature, players can find a custom Hytale server that fits whatever they’re looking for, with the feature allowing owners to put up a name description, server type, audience age tag, and region for now. 

Server operators now already have new listing tools available so that they can start refining their servers’ listings and get them verified ahead of the Update 5 launch, though Hypixel notes it’ll take a bit longer than usual for the initial launch if the applications come in heavy. These settings can be found under the “Server Profiles” page.

Features are minimal for now, and that’s by design, as it gives them the “foundation we need” to build out more capabilities. 

“Over time, we want Hytale to become a better one-stop place to explore community-made experiences that would otherwise be scattered across third-party sites and social channels,” the full announcement post explains. “Richer listings, better browsing tools, deeper customization, and more ways for great communities to be found are all directions we want to build toward after the basics are solid.” 

This will be part of the game’s fifth major update, which has started to see the sub-patches roll out on public testing servers. It looks like this update will also come with custom scripted encounters, increased storage sizes, new placement settings and more. Hytale has been launching weekly public test server updates before its major patches, with the last one, Update 4, having come out in March.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/hytale-announces-in-game-custom-server-discovery-tool-coming-in-update-5-2000137985
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: RuneScape's Player Avatar Beta Gets Slight Delay, Jagex Adds New Dyeable Gear Options
item.content:encoded:
Today was supposed to be the start of RuneScape’s player avatar beta, but Jagex needs a little longer to make sure it's ready to be tested so there has been a slight delay. We also get an idea of what else the team will be sharing this week.

The good news is that the Player Avatar beta hasn’t been delayed for too long, as it will be out sometime later this week. The announcement didn’t say exactly when, but it’s Tuesday, so we should know soon. The other piece of good news about delaying the beta a bit is that the player Avatar update launch is still scheduled for later this month.

When the beta does go live, we can expect a special dedicated blog with all the info you'll need to know as well as how to access the test.

The Road to Restoration efforts move fast, and  we should get a different dedicated blog tomorrow about next week's planned Dungeoneering update. It’s a busy week, since the team also plans to release the findings of their investigation into performance improvement possibilities.

The new update also adds dyeable gear options. The  Elite Dracolich Armor and Roar of Awakening/Ode to Deceit weapons are now customizable.

Separately, last week, RuneFest tickets went on sale. The 2026 event is the biggest ever, and Jagex is also looking for community input on proposed mid-game balance changes.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/runescapes-player-avatar-beta-gets-slight-delay-jagex-adds-new-dyeable-gear-options-2000137984
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Steam Controller Sells Out Almost Instantly, Valve States They Will Have A Restock Update 'Soon'
item.content:encoded:
It was probably inevitable, but Valve's hotly anticipated Steam Controller sold out yesterday in minutes, and the secondary market has already become flooded with scalpers looking to make a quick buck. 

Valve's new Steam Controller has been anticipated for months and thanks to some great reviews (we're still working on ours), the demand for Valve's new controller only got bigger in the lead up to launch day. So it's not surprising that, within minutes of going live, the controller was already sold out. 

Steam Controller ran out faster than we anticipated, and we hate that not everyone who wanted one was able to get it. We’re working on getting more in stock and will have an update on expected timeline soon.

— Valve (@valvesoftware) May 5, 2026

Valve admitted in a statement this morning that the controller sold faster than the company "anticipated," and that they "hate that not everyone who wanted one was able to get it." However, the company has also confirmed they are looking into getting more Controllers back in stock and will "have an update on expected timeline soon."

Meanwhile, as with everything tech in recent memory, scalpers have popped up listing the new controller at some outrageous prices, sometimes around $300 as of this writing. While some listings can be found around $220, most are sitting nearer the $300 price point, with one eBay lister putting out an exorbitant $39K offer on the site. 

The Steam Controller, not to be confused with the other Steam Controller that Valve released over ten years ago with the same exact name, features your standard controller layout, but with its own twist: two track pads below the thumbsticks to help controller players navigate mouse-heavy PC games. These trackpads are similar to what we see on the Steam Deck, but are actually more responsive in my testing, and I've found myself able to play games like EVE Online and The Lord of the Rings Online fairly well with them. We'll have our full review soon, but first impressions are definitely good. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/hardware/steam-controller-sells-out-almost-instantly-valve-states-they-will-have-a-restock-update-soon-2000137986
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: 'Operation: Belly of the Beast' Returns to Warframe, Digital Extremes Opens TennoCon Digital & Merch Pack Sales
item.content:encoded:
Warframe brings back Operation: Belly of the Beast and it's time to get your clan to work together and take on threats across the Origin System. There are new and returning rewards to collect, as well as specials for those in clans. Digital Extremes has also just put TennoCon 2026 digital and merch packs on sale.

The clan event runs through June 1st at 2pm ET, and if you’ve completed the Jade Shadows quest and The New War, then you’re ready. Join up with your clan to get started, and take on the Jade Light Eximus units that spawn instead of regular Eximus units. Help Ordis to stop Parvis via one of three options. As you continue in the associated missions, you’ll access nodes that let you take on Parvos’ forces.  

This event is also preparation for the upcoming Jade Shadows: Constellations major update. 

Operation: Belly of the Beast  has a new Brutus node on Uranus and that itself comes with a new Operation variant that will have you beating back more Jade Light Eximus units. You can choose to summon a Sister of Parvos for more Volatile Motes to aid you.

There will also be timed Operation alerts across the Origin System. These non-Ascension missions award Volatile Motes. Collect these and a community counter will rise and make progress towards Collective rewards including a new variant of the Jade Light Ephemera.

There’s more to know about the Operation, as well as the long list of rewards, both new and returning.

TennoCon 2026 will return to London, Ontario Canada in July. Digital Extremes has just opened up sales for digital and merch packs. If you have a TennoCon ticket, you’ll get these packs automatically, but for the rest of us, These are ways to get your hands on special contents including the Hunhullus Ephemera, themed emote, glyph, sigil, and display for TennoCon2026, and more. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/operation-belly-of-the-beast-returns-to-warframe-digital-extremes-opens-tennocon-digital-and-merch-pack-sales-2000137983
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
